course_description: This course provides an introduction to the fundamentals of financial accounting. The course covers the basic principles and procedures of accounting, including the accounting cycle, preparation of financial statements, and the interpretation of financial information. By the end of this course, students will be able to apply fundamental accounting principles to record business transactions, prepare and interpret basic financial statements, and analyse financial and non-financial information for decision-making purposes.

course_aims: The aim of this course is to provide students with the necessary skills to analyse and interpret financial reports. The course covers a range of topics in financial accounting including double-entry system, accounting cycle, and preparation/interpretation of financial statements. Additionally the course introduces the way that information from non-financial reporting is used for decision making.

learning_outcomes.description: Explain accounting concepts, principles and methods.
Record accounting transactions in accordance with the requirements in International Accounting Standards and Australian Accounting Standards.
Describe the regulatory framework within which International Accounting Standards and Australian Accounting Standards are developed and issued.
Develop and demonstrate effective teamwork skills by collaborating with peers to analyse and interpret financial statements, and prepare a comprehensive financial report.

assessment_details.title: In-Semester Exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The In-Semester exam consists of multiple choice questions, short answer theory questions, practical questions including the preparation of journal entries (including adjusting and closing entries), and the preparation of basic financial statements. The material will be drawn from Topics 1 to 4 inclusive . In preparing for the exam you should review all lecture material, tutorial questions and exercises. The exam will be a closed-book exam invigilated on campus and timed to 90 minutes with an additional 10 minutes perusal time. AI Statement This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Assignment: Understanding Financial Statements
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This assignment is designed to examine the application of Topic 5 Financial Statement Analysis. It will assess your report writing skills, general accounting knowledge, and ability to analyse financial and non-financial information for business decision-making. This assignment is to be completed on a group basis. AI Statement Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 14 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_activities.topic: Lecture 1 - Introduction to Financial Reporting This lecture will provide an introduction to accounting including the business environment, the regulatory environment and the financial statements.
No Tutorials No tutorials this week

Lecture 2 - Recording Transactions: Double Entry Accounting Students will learn the principle of double-entry accounting (debits and credits) and how they are used to record accounting transactions. Learning outcomes: L02, L03
Tutorial 1 Introduction to Financial Reporting & Overview of accouting software: Xero

Lecture 3 - Revenue and Expense Recognition in Accrual Accounting Students will learn the accrual accounting concepts and how they are used in making adjusting entries for prepayments and accruals. Learning outcomes: L02

Tutorial 2 Recording Transactions - Double Entry Accounting & Xero Workshop (1): Preparing journal entries and trial balance using Xero Note: There is a public holiday on 13th August (Ekka Holiday). Students who normally attend the Wednesday tutorials can attend alternate tutorials in this week. Learning outcomes: L02, L03
Lecture 4 - Accounting for Merchandising Firms Students will learn the accounting treatment of different inventory systems and cost flow methods. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05
Tutorial 3 Revenue and Expense Recognition in Accrual Accounting & Xero Workshop (2): Preparing adjusting journal entries and financial statements using Xero. Learning outcomes: L02

Lecture 5 - Financial Statement Analysis Students will learn different tools of financial statement analysis including ratio analysis for assessing a company's creditworthiness and profitability. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Tutorial 4 Accounting for Merchandising Firms Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05
Lecture 6 - Accounting for Current Assets Students will learn internal control principles for handling cash and the reporting of cash and receivables in the financial statements.
Tutorial 5 Financial Statement Analysis Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Prepare for In-Semester Exam There will be no lectures and no tutorials this week to assist with revision for the in-semester exam. Additional consultation sessions will be provided this week.

Lecture 7 - Accounting for Non-Current Assets Students will learn the accounting treatment of non-current assets including the concept of depreciation, asset impairments and revaluation. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05
Tutorial 6 Accounting for Current Assets

Lecture 8 - Liabilities Students will learn the accounting treatment of current and non-current liabilities including debentures, provisions and contingent liabilities. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05
Tutorial 7 Accounting for Non-Current Assets Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
In-Semester Break There are no lecture or workshops this week.
No Lecture (Public Holiday) King's Birthday - Monday 6 Oct 2025 Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05
Tutorial 8 Liabilities Kings Birthday Public Holiday - Monday 6 October 2025 - Check Blackboard for announcements about affected classes. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05
Lecture 9 - Equity Students will learn the accounting treatment of equity including the issuance of ordinary shares, share splits, cash dividends and share dividends. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05
No tutorials No tutorials this week.

Lecture 10 - Statement of Cash Flows Students will learn how to prepare a statement of cash flows using the direct/indirect method and how to evaluate an entity using cash flows. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05
Tutorial 9 - Equity Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L05
Lecture 11 - Revision Lecture This is a revision lecture and all the important concepts and principles learned in this course will be reviewed.
Tutorial 10 - Statement of Cash Flows Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05
